title: malus × purpurea var. eleyi
intro: Malus × purpurea var. eleyi, commonly known as the Purple Crabapple, is a hybrid plant of the Malus genus, known for its beautiful purple flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Malus × purpurea var. eleyi lie in its high content of antioxidants, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help protect against cell damage. The health benefits of consuming the fruits or using the plant in herbal preparations include reducing the risk of chronic diseases, such as heart disease and diabetes, as well as supporting digestive health and immune function. Malus × purpurea var. eleyi can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and longevity.
